seoTitle: Mornington vs Mount Direction — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Mornington and Mount Direction. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Mornington vs Mount Direction: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Mornington and Mount Direction.
heading: Mornington vs Mount Direction — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Mornington, Tasmania and Mount Direction, Tasmania. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Mornington has a lower overall climate risk score (34/100) compared to Mount Direction (37/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Mornington or Mount Direction safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Mornington has a flood risk score of 51/100, while Mount Direction scores 57/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Mornington scores 19/100 for bushfire risk. Mount Direction scores 20/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Mornington or Mount Direction?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Mornington has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.1% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
